vulnerability.title: Ensure the Logon Failure Delay is Set Correctly in login.defs
vulnerability.description: To ensure the logon failure delay controlled by /etc/login.defs is set properly, add or correct the FAIL_DELAY setting in /etc/login.defs to read as follows: FAIL_DELAY
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( rpm --quiet -q shadow-utils && rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ); then var_accounts_fail_delay='' # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^FAIL_DELAY") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_accounts_fail_delay" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -q -m 1 -i -e "^FAIL_DELAY\\>" "/etc/login.defs"; then escaped_formatted_output=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$formatted_output") LC_

triage_decision.reason: This finding modifies a single configuration parameter (FAIL_DELAY) in /etc/login.defs, which controls the delay after failed login attempts. The remediation script follows standard STIG patterns and only edits one file. This is a common hardening measure that does not affect authentication mechanisms, networking, filesystems, or require a reboot.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for accounts_logon_fail_delay (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_accounts_logon_fail_delay). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
The password expiration is blocking all commands. The remediation cannot be completed until the password issue is resolved. The final command to execute once access is restored is:

```bash
sed -i 's/^#\\?\\s*FAIL_DELAY.*/FAIL_DELAY 4/' /etc/login.defs
```

This will set the logon failure delay to 4 seconds as required by the STIG.

llm_verdict.message:
I cannot proceed with the remediation because the system is requiring an immediate password change due to password expiration. The remediation requires accessing and modifying /etc/login.defs, but I'm unable to do so because of this authentication barrier.

Please resolve the password expiration issue first (either by changing the root password interactively or by allowing password expiration to be bypassed), then I can complete the remediation for the accounts_logon_fail_delay finding by setting FAIL_DELAY to 4 seconds in /etc/login.defs.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed plan would functionally resolve the vulnerability by modifying /etc/login.defs to set FAIL_DELAY. However, the plan lacks specificity about the exact value to set and doesn't mention checking if the parameter already exists before modifying. The STIG remediation script shows a more robust approach with proper error handling and search pattern stripping.
concerns: Plan doesn't specify the exact FAIL_DELAY value to set
No mention of handling cases where the line is commented out
Lacks verification steps to confirm the change was applied successfully
suggested_improvements: Specify the exact FAIL_DELAY value (typically 4 seconds per STIG)
Include steps to uncomment the line if it's commented out
Add verification command to confirm the setting was applied correctly
Consider using the full STIG remediation script for more robust handling
